texto: I grew up with a very romantic, idealized vision of New York, probably because of all the books I read and the movies I watched.

texto: Most normal boys, as they're growing up, they - in order to become attractive, they might, you know, get good at sports or join a rock band or develop good social skills, and for some reason, I thought that drawing comic books might be my route.

texto: I don't pick up my work at all. If it's something that's still in progress and I have the chance to make some edits on the material or think about the order, little things like that, I'll keep those stories at hand and go through them. But once it exists as the book, it's locked away in a vault, and I kind of put it behind me.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Adrian Tomine: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “I don't pick up my work at all. If it's something that's still in progress and I have the chance …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, work, progress pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de trabalho e progress.

texto: When I first started drawing the earliest incarnation of 'Optic Nerve,' I hadn't even been on a date; I hadn't had a romantic relationship of any kind yet, so in a way, I was almost writing science fiction.

texto: There's a lot of books that I've purchased simply because of the cover design. On the other hand, there's certain books that, even if I'm very curious about the content, I can't bring myself to buy if I really dislike the cover.

texto: For a lot of the time I was in Berkeley, I was single. I was living in a kind of collegiate apartment by myself - it was like a protracted summer vacation. So at least in hindsight, I have gloomy emotions attached to Berkeley, whereas I started coming to New York because I was dating someone, and it was very exciting and romantic.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Adrian Tomine: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “For a lot of the time I was in Berkeley, I was single.
